summary: PASS on coder/v8. Auditor v2 major #1 closed: generator-side guard restores the MissingRequiredParameter contract for non-nullable Data<Variable> slots. Discovery detects T : IRawNameResolvable, plumbs through ActionClassInfo, Action emitter emits an eager pre-Run() check that mirrors [IsNotNull]'s shape — fires before any handler reads Name.Value, so the implicit-conversion NRE path is unreachable. 20-row parametrized regression in MissingVariableNameTests.cs pins all non-nullable variable.* + list.* slots; foreach's intentionally-nullable ItemName/KeyName excluded by !p.IsNullable filter. C# 2570/2570 + plang 166/166 green. One NIT: empty-string slot values ("") pass the new guard (which checks `?.Value == null`) but were caught pre-v7 by `string.IsNullOrEmpty(...)` — minor diagnostic gap, not a v8 regression, optional follow-up. v2 minor findings: #2 (security count) was a review-quality reflection — no code action; #3 (tester gap) closed by the new regression test. Verdict PASS.

previous_reviews_assessed.auditor_v2: v2 finding #1 fully addressed. Coder followed the recommended shape — generator-side, mirrors IsSensitive plumbing — but emitted the guard in the Action emitter rather than the Property getter. Functionally equivalent and structurally cleaner.

findings.issue: The new guard checks `__action?.Parameters.FirstOrDefault(...)?.Value == null`. Pre-v7's RawScalarValidations used `string.IsNullOrEmpty(...)`. So an explicit empty-string slot (`Name = ""`) passes the guard, resolves through Variable.Resolve to Variable{Name=""}, and Variables.Set("", ...) / Variables.Get("") silently no-op. Pre-v7 surfaced this as MissingParameter.
findings.impact: Diagnostic-fidelity gap on a rare malformed-.pr edge. Not a crash. Reachable only from LLM emission bugs or hand-edited .pr — within the signed-.pr trust boundary. Not a v8 regression (the gap predates this branch); v8 closed the dominant null/missing case.
findings.suggestion: Optional follow-up — change the condition to `?.Value is null or string s && string.IsNullOrEmpty(s)`. One-line tightening. Not blocking.
